prompt: Write the opening paragraph of a children's book about a shy octopus who wants to make friends.
response: Deep beneath the shimmering waves, where sunlight danced in golden ribbons and coral castles bloomed in every color of the rainbow, lived a little octopus named Ollie. Ollie had eight wiggly arms, eyes as big and soft as moonstones, and a heart full of wishes—but he was terribly, terribly shy. Every time the cheerful clownfish zoomed by for a game of hide-and-seek or the chatty seahorses invited him to tea in their kelp garden, Ollie would puff up with nervous bubbles and dart backward into his cozy nook behind a giant clamshell. He *wanted* to say hello, to laugh, to share his collection of shiny sea-glass treasures… but his courage always seemed to slip away like sand through his tentacles.
